channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Zimbabwe men’s volleyball claim CAVB Zone VI gold to Olympic dream alive
item.description: Zimbabwe men’s volleyball claim CAVB Zone VI gold to Olympic dream alive — via 3-mob.com #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: General Chiwenga’s Civil War Warning as Team Mnangagwa pushes for CAB3 victory: Unpacking the VP’s Dire Prophecy
item.description: Zimbabwe finds itself at a precarious crossroads, with a chilling statement attributed to a source close to Vice President Constantino Chiwenga suggesting the nation has ‘never been this close to a civil war’. This alarming pronouncement, emerging amidst the contentious Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) and persistent rumours of a deepening rift between President [...] The post General Chiwenga’s Civil War Warning as Team Mnangagwa pushes for CAB3 victory: Unpacking the VP’s Dire Prophecy first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Chaos in ZRP as Sergeant sues his boss and 5 other police officers for ‘messing up with him’
item.description: ZRP Cop VS 6 BOSSES: The Internal War Tearing Masvingo’s Police Force Apart MASVINGO – In the high-stakes world of Zimbabwean law enforcement, the thin blue line is usually expected to hold firm against the rising tide of crime. But in the corridors of the Masvingo Magistrate Courts, that line has not just thinned; it [...] The post Chaos in ZRP as Sergeant sues his boss and 5 other police officers for ‘messing up with him’ first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Tungwarara Seeks Truce with Matinyarare Through Dialogue and Vehicle Gift @263Chat
item.description: Presidential adviser Paul Tungwarara has offered a luxury vehicle to former government critic Rutendo Matinyarare as part of efforts to ease tensions following months of public criticism directed at President Emmerson Mnangagwa. The unusual gesture, which includes the offer of a Toyota Land Cruiser 300 [...] The post Tungwarara Seeks Truce with Matinyarare Through Dialogue and Vehicle Gift appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Former FIFA president Blatter once backed Zimbabwe’s World Cup dream: Ex-minister Mzembi
item.description: FORMER Tourism and Hospitality minister Walter Mzembi has revealed that Zimbabwe once pursued a bid to co-host the FIFA World Cup, claiming he secured the support of then-FIFA president Sepp Blatter for an exploratory campaign. In an X post, Mzembi said that while in government in 2010 he initiated talks with FIFA over a joint [...] The post Former FIFA president Blatter once backed Zimbabwe’s World Cup dream: Ex-minister Mzembi appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: FOOD SECURITY BOOST... Record wheat crop to fortify nation against El Niño drought threat @zimsituation
item.description: Source: FOOD SECURITY BOOST... Record wheat crop to fortify nation against El Niño drought threat – herald Professor Obert Jiri Theseus Mauruki Shambare ZIMBABWE is poised for another landmark wheat harvest that could significantly strengthen national food security, reduce depen #Zimbabwe #Climate #Environment #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: CAB3 breaks Parliament debate records as vote looms @zimsituation
item.description: Source: CAB3 breaks Parliament debate records as vote looms – herald Minister Ziyambi Ziyambi Nyore Madzianike Senior Reporter THE number of Members of Parliament who have registered to contribute to the ongoing debate on the Constitutional Amendment (No. 3) Bill (CAB3) during Tuesday’s sitting is expected to influence the timeline of when legislators will proceed [...] The post CAB3 breaks Parliament debate records as vote looms appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Zim eyes multi-million-dollar deals under AfCFTA @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Zim eyes multi-million-dollar deals under AfCFTA – herald Oliver Kazunga Senior Reporter ZIMBABWE is on the verge of formally unlocking preferential access to Africa’s vast continental market as it moves to finalise and gazette its African Continental Free Trade Area (AfC #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: What if the American violations ahead of the World Cup happened in any other country?
item.description: Even before the first whistle of the 2026 World Cup, scandals have already begun to surface. Long before reaching the pitches, we are currently stuck at airport gates and bogged down in visa complications, where the arbitrary measures and blatant violations practised by US immigration authorities reveal a narrow, security-obsessed mindset. These hostile actions completely contradict the very spirit [...] The post What if the American violations ahead of the World Cup happened in any other country? appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Matinyarare Reconciles With Mnangagwa, Accepting A Brand New Car To Stop Leaking Audio Evidence That Chamisa Won 2023 Elections

item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Illegal Foreigner’s Desperate Measures: Zimbabwean Man Found Living Inside High-Voltage Power Substation in Joburg (VIDEO)
item.description: Johannesburg – A deeply unsettling discovery in Melville, Johannesburg, has brought into sharp focus the severe socio-economic pressures driving individuals to unimaginable extremes. On or around 9th June 2026, a Zimbabwean national was found to have established a makeshift home within a live high-voltage electrical substation, a location fraught with peril. The incident, captured in [...] The post Illegal Foreigner’s Desperate Measures: Zimbabwean Man Found Living Inside High-Voltage Power Substation in Joburg (VIDEO) first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Police Hail Community Cooperation After Successful Prosecutions @263Chat @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: Several convicted criminals have been sentenced to lengthy prison terms following successful investigations by the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) with authorities crediting members of the public for providing information that led to the arrests. In a statement, national police spokesperson Commissioner Paul Nyathi announced the [...] The post Police Hail Community Cooperation After Successful Prosecutions appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Russia hails Zimbabwe’s UN Security Council election @bulawayo24
item.description: Russia has congratulated Zimbabwe on securing a non-permanent seat on the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) for the 2027-2028 term, pledging to work closely with Harare to strengthen Africa’s inf... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.dc:creator: The Herald ZW

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda
